database_name.description: Specifies the name of the database within the global cluster, must be unique within the cluster and adhere to RDS naming conventions.

deletion_protection.description: Enables or disables deletion protection for the RDS global cluster to prevent accidental deletion.

engine.description: Specifies the database engine type for the global cluster, such as Aurora. Must match the engine of associated DB clusters.

engine_lifecycle_support.description: Specifies the engine lifecycle support for the global database cluster (e.g., Aurora MySQL or Aurora PostgreSQL); must match the supported lifecycle of the chosen engine.

engine_version.description: Specifies the database engine version for the global cluster; must match a valid version for the selected database engine.

force_destroy.description: Indicates whether to allow the deletion of the global cluster even if it contains associated databases.

global_cluster_identifier.description: Unique identifier for the RDS global cluster; must be unique across all global clusters in the AWS account.

source_db_cluster_identifier.description: Identifier of the source DB cluster to use for creating the global database; must be an Amazon Aurora cluster.

storage_encrypted.description: Specifies whether storage encryption is enabled for the global database cluster. Must be true for encrypted clusters.

tags.description: Key-value pairs to organize and categorize resources; requires unique keys and supports filtering in AWS services.

timeouts.description: Defines the duration for specific operations such as creation, update, and deletion of the global database cluster.
